taxonomy: Phalangium laxum is classified as a species of whip scorpion in the order Thelyphonida, which is a group of arachnids related to spiders and scorpions. It belongs to the family Phalangiidae and is native to certain parts of the world. The classification of this species is based on its physical characteristics and morphological features. Phalangium laxum is often found in humid environments and is part of the arachnid fauna. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of this species.
uses: Phalangium laxum, a species of whip scorpion, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes.
